title: Blood Lactate Measurement
content_markdown:
#### Indications

- Patient displaying signs of infection or septic shock.

#### Procedure

- Remove the test strip from the packaging and insert into the lactate device.

- Select a finger and clean with an alcohol swab.

- Allow the finger to dry.

- Using a lancet, pierce the skin and squeeze a small amount of blood from the wound.

- Dispose of the used lancet appropriately.

- Wipe away the first drop of blood (it can be used for BGL testing).

- Place a sample of blood on the test strip until the lactate device beeps and a countdown appears.

- Apply a Band-Aid to the wound.

- Wait for the test result, and record the lactate measurement.

- Dispose of the used test strip appropriately.

#### Specific Notes

- A “LO” or “HIGH” reading indicates a lactate <0.5mmol /L or >25mmol/L.
- Excessive exercise, movement of the limb, suspected crush injury or compartment syndrome of the limb to be tested may produce inaccurate results.
